response:
We tightened SyncEventGrid to only include Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions rather than all customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks.

Concretely:
- SyncEventGrid now joins customer_subscriptions → lighthouse_delegations and only scopes subscriptions where lighthouse_delegations.managed_by_tenant_id equals the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state is active or succeeded.
- Event Grid rows are filtered by this ownership rule before being upserted into inventory.
- We intentionally continue to use the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential (we do NOT acquire per‑customer tenant tokens).
context_injected: {"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "compliance", "facts": ["automation_policies_topic_trigger depends_on azr-fleet-platform-events", "azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall targets azr-compliance-v3", "azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall contains AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_CREATE_REQUESTED", "Fleet contains parse_automation_rule_event", "parse_automation_rule_event depends_on governance bulk_automation_items", "AutomationRuleEvent contains governance_message_id", "eventgrid_resource_change_webhook deployed_to fdda1087-ec7e-45ee-ac50-3c34ff7cc6fc"], "recent": ["2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upser", "2026-04-09: For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.\n[specifics: repository: azr-compliance-v3; pr_number: 72; c", "2026-04-09: Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/reme", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFOR", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explic", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 in fleet-platform-app, investigated why global Event Grid filters were not excluding Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces in prod. Live Azure event subscription rax-resource-change-1193525 on subscription 025d123f-b6ac-470a-a420-bf4d3087670f had advanced filters on key data.resourceInfo.type, but a captured raw EventGridSchema ResourceWriteSuccess payload showed subject and data.resourceUri fields, not data.resourceInfo.type. This made the filter ineffective even though it was deployed. Patched functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py to use Subject-based StringNotContains filters wi", "2026-03-23: Implemented Event Grid name reconciliation in fleet-platform-app on 2026-03-23. Canonical Event Grid subscription name remains rax-resource-change-{customer_id[:12]}. Added shared classification utilities for live subscription-scoped Event Grid resources in shared/eventgrid_utils.py, including extraction of resource name, webhook URL, delivery attributes, and classification into canonical / legacy Fleet-targeting / external. Hardened postgres operational reads to be deterministic with ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC and added list_eventgrid_subscription_records(subscription_id). Updated ensu", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 for azr-fleet-platform PR #72, three live GitHub review threads were fetched and all were valid. Fixes landed in commit 201178f on dev. First, clarified the comment in functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py to state that subscription-scoped EventGridSchema/CloudEvents use top-level subject and optional data.resourceUri, not data.resourceInfo.type. Second, added runtime normalization in resolve_eventgrid_filter_profile() so legacy DB overrides using data.resourceInfo.type are converted to Subject-based StringNotContains filters with normalized path fragments, preserving existing ov", "2026-03-20: Implemented customer/subscription-specific Event Grid filter override support in fleet-platform-app on 2026-03-20. Added Postgres table eventgrid_filter_overrides via migration 034 and schema sync, PgStore methods resolve_eventgrid_filter_override and upsert_eventgrid_filter_override, global default filters for Event Grid subscriptions, override resolution precedence subscription > customer > global default in functions/activities/eventgrid.py, eventTypes parameter support in the ARM event subscription template, and token-rotation preservation of eventTypes/advancedFilters in functions/trigger"]}
